description: A consumer retrieves a context, verifies the body end-to-end (content_hash recomputes correctly AND the producer signature verifies), then fetches an external data_ref.location. The fetched bytes do NOT match the producer-declared data_ref.content_hash. This is a DataRef-level integrity failure at the data layer — the body itself remains cryptographically valid. The SDK SHOULD surface data_ref_hash_mismatch, NOT invalid_signature (which would wrongly imply a signature failure) and NOT hash_mismatch (which would wrongly imply the whole body is unverifiable). See RFC-ACDP-0007 §5 'Distinguishing hash failures' and §5.3 'SDK guidance'. This is the external/consumer-side counterpart to data-ref-007 (embedded/registry-side).

input.scenario: Consumer-side verification after fetching an external data_ref.location
input.verified_body: The retrieved context body verifies fully: recomputed content_hash equals body.content_hash, and body.signature verifies against the producer's resolved public key.

data_ref_under_test._declared_hash_note: The producer signed a body that declares this content_hash for the referenced CSV.
data_ref_under_test._fetched_bytes_note: The bytes actually served at the location now hash to a different value — the upstream data store mutated the object after publication.

expected.rationale: The producer signed the hash of the data they intended to reference; the data at that location has since changed. The integrity failure is at the data-reference layer, not the body layer. An SDK verification result SHOULD expose the body-level verdict (valid) and the data-ref-level verdict (data_ref_hash_mismatch) as separate fields so the application can still trust the body's metadata and derived_from lineage while treating this one DataRef as unusable.
expected.consumer_behavior: SHOULD. Consumers fetching data_refs[].location MUST treat the result as untrusted until verified against data_ref.content_hash (RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.5). On mismatch the consumer SHOULD surface the data_ref_hash_mismatch semantic in its diagnostics, logs, or API surface.
